mnemonics: CPR: Cartesian Product = all Possible Relations. It's the set of every ordered pair from the first set with every element from the second set.
A Relation is a Rule - It's a subset of the Cartesian Product, selecting which pairs follow a specific rule or condition.
keyPoints: The Cartesian Product of sets A and B (A × B) is the set of all ordered pairs (a, b) where a ∈ A and b ∈ B.
A Relation R from set A to set B is any subset of the Cartesian Product A × B.
Relations can have properties like reflexivity, symmetry, antisymmetry, and transitivity, which define special types like equivalence relations and partial orders.
commonMistakes: Confusing an ordered pair (a, b) with the unordered set {a, b}. Order matters in a Cartesian Product; (a, b) ≠ (b, a) unless a = b.
Forgetting that a relation is defined on a set. Stating 'this relation is symmetric' is meaningless without specifying the underlying set it is defined on.
Assuming the Cartesian Product is commutative. A × B is not the same as B × A unless A = B.
